title: teucrium genevense
intro: Teucrium genevense, also known as Alpine Tea or Mountain Tea, is a plant species native to the European Alps, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and woody stems. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Alpine Tea is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant has been found to have potential health benefits, including soothing digestive problems, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Alpine Tea is often prepared as a tea by infusing the dried leaves in hot water, and it can also be found in capsule or tincture form in some herbal health products.
